reviews.review_msg: Brotato still aint give marks back and its been so long. He marked the first memo and thats all we got for the semester. Lectures are okay, midterm is pretty easy. Overall decent, just doesnt mark assignments right away so you never know how youre really doing in the course.

Similar to what the other students say, Anthony is very nice and is kind.  The COURSE is tedious.  Each week: 50+ pgs of reading, case study, study guide, blog post, blog reviews, assignments (3+ parts to everything), and STILL NOT A SINGLE GRADE BACK one week post the last class. I would not take this class again, nor another class with Anthony.
Anthony is a great and engaging prof. His classes are interesting, and he explains things clearly. Just be prepared for lots of writing. Overall, really good prof!

Anthony is an engaging and insightful lecturer. Unfortunately, this class is bogged down by the massive number of assignments and readings. He assigns 50-150 pages of reading every week, on top of a weekly 500 word reflection post, two presentations, a debate, a paper, and two feedback papers. Oh, and we haven't gotten a SINGLE grade back!!

Insightful. Enjoyed his lectures

1 midterm (solely on tb materials; mcq)
1 group project (30 pg+; pick team wisely)
A lot of memos (500 words; 12 cases)
2 individual assignments (20 pg+)

Participation matters; make or break your letter grade - just talk when you can

No final

Grading criteria not entirely clear

Anthony is a good professor, but assigns crazy amounts of work. Weekly 500-word memos two 3000-word assignments a 30-page report and a 90-question MCQ exam. Participation 20%! First assignment wasn't marked before the second was released + he stopped marking memos after 5th week. Disorganized and stubborn.

TAKE THE CLASS IF YOU LOVE WRITING ONLY!!

Anthony cares about his students' success but the class is brutally tedious. Weekly readings, blog posts, assignments, written critiques, and the two presentations demand both feedback and group essays. There's a ton of homework, but it's not hard. The semester ended a month ago, and we still only have marks for part 1 of the first assignment.

He is a great prof! The class on the other hand is rather unnecessary. Slow start to the class but at the end you end up with a lot of assignments. He doesn't like marking anything for some reason though. A week before the finals for other classes and he has released one memo we wrote in the second week of class.

Class consisted of two individual written assignments, a group assignment, a final paper, and a weekly memo. At the end of the semester only the memos from the first two weeks of class were marked along with the first individual assignment. Very unreasonably workload which along with the lack of feedback, left many students demoralized.

Anthony is a great lecturer, he is able to clearly explain concepts and guide students to develop their own critical thinking through the use of HBR cases. However, throughout the span of the semester, we had a total of 14 assignments, of which we only received feedback on 3/14 by the end of the term, so no feedback for improvement.
